evidence.scientific_clarity: All four panels (pipeline diagram, ablation bars, training curves, heatmap) clearly communicate the method overview and empirical results as specified in the brief; labels A-D are present and bold sans-serif.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 is NOT set in gen_figure.py; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (non-editable, bitmapped) DejaVu Sans fonts — the default Matplotlib font — instead of Type 42 selectable text.
evidence.palette: Blue (#4C72B0) and light-blue (#9DB8D2) palette is colorblind-safe; Viridis colormap for heatmap is excellent; no misuse of red/green for directional encoding.
evidence.layout_density: gridspec(2,3,width_ratios=[2,1,1]) matches brief; dominant Panel A, smaller Panels B-C, and full-width Panel D all fill their space without gaping whitespace or overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content well; legend placement in Panel C is clean; however, Panel A label appears twice (once via 'Panel A' text inside axes, once via 'A' in transAxes coords), a minor redundancy a reviewer might flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is real vector (not a rasterized image), but fonts are Type 3 (non-selectable) because pdf.fonttype=42 was never set; text is not selectable/editable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: All required outputs exist and are non-trivial: figure.pdf (46 kB), figure.png (124 kB), figure.svg (99 kB), caption.tex (789 B), gen_figure.py (6.7 kB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains what each panel shows with specific findings: full model score of 64.7 in Panel B and heatmap score range 0.04–0.99 in Panel D provide concrete numerical claims.

overall_strengths: Well-structured 4-panel layout matching the brief exactly (gridspec 2×3 with correct width_ratios), with all required scientific content clearly presented.
Caption is thorough and specific, including numerical claims (64.7 score, 0.04–0.99 heatmap range) and clear per-panel descriptions; colorblind-safe palette with Viridis heatmap.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) are not set, so the PDF contains Type 3 bitmapped fonts — text is not selectable/editable, a critical omission for journal-quality vector figures.

Panel A label is redundant (both an in-axes 'Panel A' text annotation and a transAxes 'A' label), and the default DejaVu Sans font is used rather than a publication-quality font such as Helvetica or Arial.
